mentions.note: Reports Tuesday; quarters poorly received despite consistent beats. Pattern suggests AI disruption possible (like Adobe, Salesforce). Down 13 days in a row—Cramer won't fight the tape.
Online payroll/HR software facing AI disruption; Claude can replicate offerings. Down 35% in 12 months, off 75% from 2021 highs. Removed from S&P 500.

CEO Gibson reported strong SMB growth, Payor acquisition bullish; but macro morass flips narrative. At 17x P/E (down from 30x), 4.6% yield, stock could compress further on recession fears. No catalyst to close gap; don't buy.
